cards.back: A technique used in operating systems where a copy of a file is made only when the file is modified, rather than making a full copy each time the file is accessed.
Reduces overhead of file system, improves performance, and conserves disk space.
Can lead to fragmentation of disk space if not implemented correctly, and may not be suitable for applications that require frequent file updates.
In file systems where data is mostly read and rarely written, such as in virtual file systems or read-only file systems.
